core_mechanism: a solution-adaptive nonlinear limiter: a derived scalar monitor field is conditionally clipped (bounded) or low-pass filtered only where local excursions signal a sharp transition, selectively injecting controlled dissipation to suppress spurious oscillations while leaving smooth regions untouched
brief_reason: The abstract builds limiters that 'erase spurious oscillations without blurring of shocks' and 'do not affect smooth solutions' by trimming (restricting) or filtering a scalar quantity, explicitly playing the same role as flux limiters in other discretizations.
